Container.Name: StreamAssets
Container.Overview: Self-hosted Twitch stream assets for OBS. Cyberpunk dark-purple design. Includes alerts overlay (follows, subs, gift subs, resubs, bits, raids) and chat overlay. Add /alerts.html and /chat.html as Browser Sources in OBS. Visit /setup after deploy to connect your Twitch account.
Container.Description: Self-hosted Twitch stream assets for OBS. Cyberpunk dark-purple design. Alerts and chat overlays.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the stream assets server. All OBS Browser Sources and the setup page use this port.

Config.Name: Data Path
Config.Description: Persistent storage for Twitch auth tokens. Tokens are saved here so they survive container restarts.

Config.Name: Twitch Client ID
Config.Description: Client ID from your Twitch Developer App. Get it at dev.twitch.tv/console/apps.

Config.Name: Twitch Client Secret
Config.Description: Client Secret from your Twitch Developer App. Keep this private — it is masked in the UI.

Config.Name: Twitch Channel
Config.Description: Your Twitch channel name, lowercase. Example: emenblade

Config.Name: Host URL
Config.Description: Full URL where this container is reachable on your network. Used for the Twitch OAuth redirect and the OBS source URLs. Update the port if you changed it above.

Config.Name: OAuth Callback URL
Config.Description: Only needed for initial Twitch auth. If your HOST_URL is HTTP (local IP), Twitch requires an HTTPS redirect URI. Set this to your HTTPS URL (e.g. a Cloudflare Tunnel or Nginx Proxy URL) just for setup. After you complete the /setup auth flow, you can clear this field — tokens are saved and this is no longer needed.

Config.Name: Spotify Client ID
Config.Description: Client ID from your Spotify Developer app. Get it at developer.spotify.com/dashboard.

Config.Name: Spotify Client Secret
Config.Description: Client Secret from your Spotify Developer app. Keep this private.

Config.Name: Spotify Callback URL
Config.Description: HTTPS redirect URI for Spotify OAuth. Must match exactly what you set in your Spotify app. Example: https://yourdomain.com/auth/spotify/callback

Config.Name: Port (internal)
Config.Description: Internal port the Node.js server listens on. Only change this if you have a port conflict inside the container. Leave blank to use the default (3000).

Config.Name: Data Directory (internal)
Config.Description: Path inside the container where tokens are stored. Leave blank to use the default (/app/data).
